name: Louisiana Housing Corporation
description_short: Louisiana Housing Corporation is a state agency offering homeownership programs, affordable housing development funding, and HUD-approved homebuyer counseling to Louisiana residents.
description_long:
Louisiana Housing Corporation (LHC) is a state agency established to expand housing opportunities across Louisiana through financing, counseling, and development programs. Operating under state authority at lhc.la.gov, LHC functions as a public housing finance agency rather than a traditional lender or for-profit company.

LHC offers a comprehensive suite of homeownership programs including MRB (Mortgage Revenue Bond) programs, the Premier Program, Delta 100 Program, and Pathways to Homeownership Program. Beyond homeownership, they provide housing development funding through sources like LIHTC (Low-Income Housing Tax Credits), HOME grants, and multifamily revenue bonds. The agency also administers homebuyer counseling services, energy assistance programs, rental assistance, homelessness solutions, and disaster recovery housing programs. They maintain compliance monitoring, asset management, and community development initiatives.

LHC distinguishes itself as a public sector housing authority rather than a commercial lender. Their programs target affordable housing and underserved populations, including veterans, non-elderly disabled individuals, and disaster survivors. They provide free homebuyer counseling, publish daily mortgage rates for transparency, and offer multiple pathways to homeownership rather than a single product.

As a state agency, LHC operates within government constraints and bureaucratic processes. Applicants should expect longer timelines than private lenders and must meet specific eligibility requirements tied to income, geography, and program-specific criteria. Their primary value lies in affordable financing options and counseling support rather than speed or convenience.
pros: Offers free HUD-approved homebuyer counseling services
Multiple affordable homeownership programs (Premier, Delta 100, Pathways) serving different borrower profiles
MRB programs provide below-market mortgage rates for qualified first-time homebuyers
Comprehensive housing development funding including LIHTC and HOME grants for affordable rental housing
Administers energy assistance and weatherization programs to reduce housing costs
Provides rental assistance and homelessness solutions beyond homeownership
Transparent daily mortgage rates published on website for rate shopping
cons: As a state agency, subject to bureaucratic processes and slower approval timelines than private lenders
Eligibility requirements tied to income limits, geographic location, and first-time homebuyer status restrict access
Limited to serving Louisiana residents only; not available nationally
Website provides program menu but limited specific details on rates, terms, or application requirements
Focus on affordable housing means programs may not serve borrowers seeking jumbo loans or investment properties
best_for: First-time Louisiana homebuyers seeking below-market mortgage rates and free counseling
Low-to-moderate income households qualifying for affordable housing programs
Disaster survivors and homelessness populations seeking emergency housing assistance
Non-profit developers and CHDOs seeking multifamily housing development funding
diagnosis: Louisiana Housing Corporation is best for first-time Louisiana homebuyers with moderate-to-low incomes seeking affordable financing paired with free counseling, and for nonprofit developers accessing multifamily housing funding. The main caveat is that this is a state agency subject to eligibility restrictions, geographic limitations, and slower processing timelines than private lenders.
services: MRB mortgage programs with below-market rates
LIHTC (Low-Income Housing Tax Credits) funding for developers
Homelessness solutions and permanent supportive housing
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
